It is a great comfort to believers that, even amidst destroyers and destruction, there is a Mediator, a great High Priest, who has an interest in heaven, and in whom saints on earth have an interest. The representation of the Divine glory from above the ark, moved to the threshold, indicated that the Lord was about to leave his mercy seat and pronounce judgment on the people. The distinguishing characteristic of this remnant that is to be saved is that they sigh and cry to God in prayer because of the abominations in Jerusalem. Those who keep pure during times of general wickedness, God will keep safe during times of general trouble and distress.

The slaughter must begin at the sanctuary so that all may see and know that the Lord hates sin most in those nearest to him. He who was appointed to protect reported the matter. Christ is faithful to the trust placed in him. Is he commanded by his Father to secure eternal life for the chosen remnant? He says, ‘Of all that you have given me, I have lost none.’ If others perish and we are saved, we must attribute the difference entirely to the mercy of our God, because we too have deserved wrath. Let us continue to plead in behalf of others. But where the Lord shows no mercy, he does no injustice; he only repays people for their actions.
